pr.title: collision related cleanup, renames, and more
pr.author: Giga-Bowser

summary: Large collision/map cleanup centered on mpColl and mpLib. The PR replaced many address-based collision symbols, prototypes, flags, globals, struct names, and field names with semantic names, then propagated those names through fighter, item, stage, and config callsites. It also centralized/renamed collision env flag macros and documented one expected regression because mpFloorGetRight had previously been a fake match.
what_changed: Renamed many mpColl/mpLib functions in symbols.txt and headers from address placeholders to semantic collision names, including mpCollCheckBounding, mpColl_SetECBSource_JObj, mpColl_SetECBSource_Fixed, mpColl_LoadECB_JObj, mpColl_LoadECB_Fixed, mpColl_LoadECB, mpCollInterpolateECB, mpCollSetFacingDir, mpCollSqueezeHorizontal, mpCollSqueezeVertical, mpCollGetSpeedCeiling, mpCollGetSpeedLeftWall, mpCollGetSpeedRightWall, and mpCollGetSpeedFloor.

Renamed map query APIs from mpLib_800... placeholders to directional check names such as mpCheckFloor, mpCheckFloorRemap, mpCheckCeiling, mpCheckCeilingRemap, mpCheckLeftWall, mpCheckLeftWallRemap, mpCheckRightWall, mpCheckRightWallRemap, mpCheckMultiple, mpCheckAllRemap, and mpCheckAll.

Renamed line and geometry helpers, including mpLineIntersection, mpLineIntersectionH, mpLineIntersectionV, mpLineNextNonFloor, mpLinePrevNonFloor, mpLinePrevNonCeiling, mpLineNextNonCeiling, mpLineNextNonLeftWall, mpLinePrevNonLeftWall, mpLinePrevNonRightWall, mpLineNextNonRightWall, mpLinesConnected, and floor/ceiling/wall endpoint accessors such as mpFloorGetRight and mpRightWallGetBottom.

Renamed dynamic joint-related functions and globals, including mpJointHide, mpJointUnhide, mpJointUpdateDynamics, mpJointUpdateBounding, mpJointListAdd, mpJointListUnlink, mpJointSetB10, jointListStart, and jointListEnd.

Moved/standardized collision env flag names in src/common_structs.h from MPCOLL_* and bit-number placeholders to Collide_* names such as Collide_LeftWallPush, Collide_LeftWallHug, Collide_LeftWallMask, Collide_RightWallPush, Collide_RightWallHug, Collide_RightWallMask, Collide_WallMask, Collide_CeilingMask, Collide_FloorMask, Collide_LeftLedgeGrab, Collide_RightLedgeGrab, and Collide_LedgeGrabMask.

Renamed map data structs and fields in mp forward/types headers: mpLib_Line became MapLine, CollInfo became MapJoint, mpCollData became MapCollData; fields like is_empty/flags became hi_flags/lo_flags, x14/x18/x1C/x20 became left_bound/bottom_bound/right_bound/top_bound, x24/x28 became joints/joint_count, and CollJoint bounding fields became bounding_min/bounding_max.

Propagated the new collision function and flag names through many fighters, items, and stages, including Captain Falcon, Ness, Pikachu, Zelda, Mewtwo, Sheik, Fox, Peach, Luigi, Popo/Nana, Link items, stage files, and generic fighter/item code.
Renamed Fighter_8006C27C to Fighter_procMap in symbols.txt, indicating map/collision update semantics for that fighter procedure.
Renamed collision state globals such as mpColl_804D649C to mpColl_IsEcbTiny and mpLib_804D64B0 to didCheckBounding.
No review comments were present in the slice; the only human note says mpFloorGetRight was a fake match before and that the regression should be ignored.
smart_moves: Kept address comments and symbol addresses stable while replacing placeholder names, making the cleanup searchable without losing binary anchoring.
Introduced consistent directional naming across floors, ceilings, left walls, and right walls, which makes collision callsites easier to audit than mpLib_800... or bit-number names.
Separated generic checks from remapped variants with a clear Remap suffix, avoiding opaque duplicated function names for similar collision tests.
Grouped collision env flags by surface type and behavior using Push, Hug, Mask, Edge, LedgeGrab, and LedgeSlip terms, which is more reusable than raw MPCOLL_FLAGS_Bxx names.
Promoted local collision flag definitions out of mpcoll.c into common_structs.h so fighter/item/stage callsites could use the same semantic names.
Renamed structs and fields only where the diff showed clear map/collision meaning, while leaving unknown fields as xNN when semantics were not established.
Called out the mpFloorGetRight fake-match regression explicitly instead of hiding it, preserving context for future matching audits.
decomp_lessons: When a subsystem has many address-named functions, rename families together: symbols.txt, headers, implementation files, and all callsites should move in one PR to prevent mixed naming from spreading.

For collision code, encode surface direction and purpose in names: CheckFloor, CheckCeiling, CheckLeftWall, CheckRightWall, GetSpeedFloor, and Next/PrevNonFloor are more reviewable than address suffixes.
Use mask names for env_flags instead of individual bit-number placeholders when the code tests grouped semantics; this makes conditions in fighter collision callbacks much easier to read.
Do not overclaim unknown data: this PR improved known fields such as MapJoint bounds and CollJoint bounding_min/bounding_max while retaining xNN names where meaning was still unclear.
Document fake matches and expected regressions in PR comments; future agents should search PR discussion before treating a regression as accidental.
A broad rename cleanup can materially improve decomp progress even without new full matches because it exposes the intent of repeated collision patterns across fighters, items, and stages.
naming_conventions: Collision env flags use Collide_<Surface><Behavior> and Collide_<Surface>Mask, for example Collide_LeftWallPush, Collide_RightWallHug, Collide_CeilingMask, and Collide_FloorMask.
Ledge and edge flags use Collide_LeftEdge, Collide_RightEdge, Collide_Edge, Collide_LeftLedgeGrab, Collide_RightLedgeGrab, Collide_LedgeGrabMask, Collide_LeftLedgeSlip, and Collide_RightLedgeSlip.
Map collision structs use Map* names for global map data structures: MapLine, MapJoint, and MapCollData.
Collision query functions use mpCheck<Surface> and mpCheck<Surface>Remap for direct and remapped floor/ceiling/wall checks.
Line traversal helpers use mpLineNextNon<Surface> and mpLinePrevNon<Surface>.
Line intersection helpers distinguish axis-specialized versions with H and V suffixes: mpLineIntersectionH and mpLineIntersectionV.
ECB setup/load functions distinguish source kind with JObj and Fixed suffixes: mpColl_SetECBSource_JObj, mpColl_SetECBSource_Fixed, mpColl_LoadECB_JObj, and mpColl_LoadECB_Fixed.
Dynamic collision joint APIs use mpJoint* prefixes, such as mpJointHide, mpJointUnhide, mpJointUpdateDynamics, mpJointUpdateBounding, mpJointListAdd, and mpJointListUnlink.
Known global booleans were renamed semantically, for example mpColl_IsEcbTiny and didCheckBounding.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The diff is primarily semantic renaming and prototype/callsite cleanup rather than evidence of new low-level matching tricks.
Preserving symbol addresses while renaming functions allows matching status and mapfile lookup to survive large cleanup changes.
The author explicitly noted that mpFloorGetRight was previously a fake match, so a regression around that symbol should not be treated as a new behavioral break from this PR.
Semantic parameter names such as line_id_skip, joint_id_skip, joint_id_only, int_x, and int_y in mplib.h can help future matching by revealing repeated call signatures and argument roles.

review_feedback.author: Giga-Bowser
review_feedback.text: `mpFloorGetRight` was a fake match before, pay no mind to the regression
review_feedback.lesson: Check PR comments for expected matching regressions before investigating a renamed collision helper.

review_feedback.text: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
review_feedback.lesson: Reviewer intent beyond the author note is not evidenced here.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching mpFloorGetRight to see how the fake-match regression was resolved.
Search for remaining mpLib_800 or mpColl_800 placeholders in collision code after PR 1993.
Search for remaining MPCOLL_* macros or MPCOLL_FLAGS_Bxx names to complete Collide_* migration.
Audit uses of Collide_LeftWallMask and Collide_RightWallMask for direction semantics because the renamed flags are widely propagated through fighter code.
Compare later MapLine, MapJoint, and MapCollData field names to see whether xNN fields gained semantics.
